title: Haptoglobin as a biomarker for cerebral palsy in newborns
abstract: A method of diagnosing a newborn as having an increased risk of developing cerebral palsy is described. The method includes obtaining a biological sample from the newborn; determining the level of Hp and/or HpRP in the biological sample and comparing it to a control value; determining the level of IL-6 in the biological sample and comparing it to a control value; and diagnosing the newborn as having an increased risk of developing cerebral palsy if the level of Hp and/or HpRP is lower than the control value, and the level of IL-6 is greater than the control value. Also described is a method of diagnosing a newborn as having an increased risk of developing retinopathy of prematurity.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of diagnosing a newborn as having an increased risk of developing cerebral palsy, comprising obtaining a biological sample from the newborn; determining the level of Hp and/or HpRP in the biological sample and comparing it to a control value; determining the level of IL-6 in the biological sample and comparing it to a control value; and diagnosing the newborn as having an increased risk of developing cerebral palsy if the level of Hp and/or HpRP is lower than the control value, and the level of IL-6 is greater than the control value. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the newborn is premature. 3. The method of claim 1, further comprising determining the level of C-reactive protein (CRP) and comparing it to a control value. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the level of Hp and/or HpRP and the level of IL-6 are determined using an immunoassay. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the immunoassay is a surface-enhancing Raman scattering (SERS) immunoassay. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the biological sample is a blood sample. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the blood sample is obtained from umbilical cord. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the biological sample is a decidua sample or a placental sample. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising providing treatment for the newborn having an increased risk of having or developing cerebral palsy. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the biological sample is obtained within 72 hours after the birth of the newborn. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein the control value for IL-6 is a value of about 100 pg/mL. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein newborn was diagnosed as having been exposed to infection in utero. 13. A kit for diagnosing a newborn as having an increased risk of developing cerebral palsy, comprising a first antibody or binding fragment thereof specific for Hp and/or HpRP, a second antibody or binding fragment thereof specific for IL-6, reagents for conducting the diagnosis, and a package for holding the antibodies and the reagents. 14. The kit of claim 13, wherein the kit further comprises surface-enhanced Raman scattering probes. 15. The kit of claim 13, wherein the kit further comprises a lateral flow strip. 16. The kit of claim 13, wherein the kit further comprises instructions for using the kit to determine the levels of Hp and/or HpRP and the level of IL-6, and Hp and/or HpRP and IL-6 controls. 17. A method of diagnosing a newborn as having an increased risk of developing retinopathy of prematurity, comprising obtaining a biological sample from the newborn; determining the level of Hp 1-2 and/or Hp2-2 in the biological sample and comparing it to a control value; and diagnosing the newborn as having an increased risk of developing retinopathy of prematurity if the level of Hp 1-2 and/or Hp2-2 is higher than the control value. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the newborn is premature. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein the level of Hpl-2 and/or Hp2-2 are determined using an immunoassay. 20. The method of claim 17, wherein the biological sample is a blood sample. 21. The method of claim 17, wherein the blood sample is obtained from umbilical cord. 22. The method of claim 17, further comprising providing treatment for the newborn having an increased risk of having or developing retinopathy of prematurity. 23. The method of claim 17, wherein the biological sample is obtained within 72 hours after the birth of the newborn.
